message:
feat(process): add async Command/Child methods for cooperative polling

Extend the process module with non-blocking async alternatives to the
synchronous output()/status()/wait() methods:

- Command::output_async(): captures stdout/stderr via cooperative polling
  instead of blocking the executor thread
- Command::status_async(): spawns a child and awaits exit via wait_async()
- Child::wait_async(): loops try_wait() + yield_now() for cooperative
  process exit detection without blocking
- Child::wait_with_output_async(): non-blocking stdout/stderr pipe draining
  with a drain_nonblocking helper that yields between read attempts

Adds integration tests verifying async output capture (echo) and non-zero
exit code propagation (exit 42) with real subprocesses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
